_meta.purpose: Curated learning library for pharmaceutical data science. Resources are hand-picked for people with a pharma/life-science background who are learning data science. Confidence is high for the listed URLs at the time of review. If a link breaks, please update via PR.

starterPaths.title: Pharma → Python in 8 weeks
starterPaths.blurb: If you've never written code and want a working data-science toolkit. Pick this if your interest is registry/clinical data, ML, or general scripting.

steps.label: Reproducible workflow
steps.note: Use the Git lessons; ignore R-specific bits.

steps.label: Domain track
steps.note: Or Galaxy / Bioconductor depending on your area.

starterPaths.title: Pharma → R for stats and pharmacometrics
starterPaths.blurb: If you'll mostly work with clinical/registry data, biostats, or pharmacometrics. R is dominant in those communities.

steps.label: Statistics intuition
steps.note: Watch alongside the next two steps.

starterPaths.title: Teacher track — building a pharma-DS module
starterPaths.blurb: If you teach pharmacy and want to add data-science elements to a course. Focus on FAIR materials you can reuse.

categories.title: Programming foundations
categories.blurb: Start here if you've never written code. Pick Python or R based on what your colleagues use.

categories.title: Statistics & data analysis
categories.blurb: Build statistical intuition before reaching for ML. Pharmacy curricula typically cover the basics; these go deeper.

categories.title: Reproducibility & workflow
categories.blurb: Git, notebooks, FAIR data. The infrastructure that turns one-off scripts into shareable science.

categories.title: Bioinformatics & Galaxy
categories.blurb: For omics, sequencing, registry/structured biological data. Galaxy is point-and-click, the rest are programmatic.

categories.title: Cheminformatics & drug discovery
categories.blurb: Working with molecules, structures, properties, and target prediction in code.

categories.title: Machine learning & AI for healthcare
categories.blurb: From classical ML (scikit-learn) through deep learning, with a focus on healthcare applications and responsible practice.

categories.title: Pharmacometrics & PKPD
categories.blurb: Population pharmacokinetic / pharmacodynamic modelling. The dialect of clinical pharmacology and dose-finding.

categories.title: Data visualisation
categories.blurb: Charts that read well in papers, posters, and dashboards. Books and reference sites, mostly free.

categories.title: Regulatory, ethics & data governance
categories.blurb: GxP, GDPR/HIPAA, fairness, validation. Necessary context for anyone building tools that touch patients.

resources.title: Python for Everybody
resources.blurb: The single best on-ramp from no programming to working Python — kind, paced, with auto-graded exercises. Free at py4e.com; same content also available as a paid Coursera Specialization with a certificate.

resources.title: A Whirlwind Tour of Python
resources.blurb: A fast, focused intro for people who already program in some other language and just want the Python they need to read scientific code.

resources.title: Real Python (selected free tutorials)
resources.blurb: Topical, readable Python tutorials. The free articles alone cover most of what you need; the paid track adds video courses.

resources.title: R for Data Science (2nd ed.)
resources.provider: Hadley Wickham, Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel & Garrett Grolemund
resources.blurb: The canonical free R + tidyverse book. Best starting point for R if you'll do data wrangling, biostats, or pharmacometrics.

resources.title: swirl — Learn R, in R
resources.blurb: Teaches R inside the R console with hands-on prompts. Pair it with R for Data Science when you want practice rather than reading.

resources.title: Posit (RStudio) Cheatsheets & Recipes
resources.blurb: One-page printable cheatsheets for ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, lubridate, and the rest of the tidyverse. The fastest reference once you know the basics.

resources.title: StatQuest with Josh Starmer
resources.blurb: Visual, intuition-first explanations of every statistical and ML concept you're likely to meet — bias-variance, regularisation, PCA, ROC curves, transformers. The friendliest single resource for self-study stats.

resources.title: Introduction to Statistical Learning (ISLR)
resources.blurb: The most-used graduate-level intro to statistical learning. Free PDF, with separate R and Python lab books that walk through the methods on real data.

resources.title: Statistical Rethinking
resources.blurb: A complete Bayesian-stats course. The book is paid but the full lecture series, code, and homework are free. The clearest treatment of causal inference + multilevel models you'll find at this level.

resources.title: Modern Statistics with R
resources.blurb: Practical, applied stats with tidyverse syntax. Covers regression, mixed models, survival analysis, and Bayesian methods.

resources.title: 3Blue1Brown — Essence of Linear Algebra / Calculus / Probability
resources.blurb: Animated math explainers with unmatched visual intuition. The 'Essence of' series is the recommended primer before any ML course.

resources.title: Pro Git
resources.blurb: The official Git book. Free, comprehensive, and stays current with the tool itself.

resources.title: GitHub Skills
resources.blurb: Hands-on Git/GitHub exercises run inside real repositories. The fastest way to learn pull requests, code review, and Actions without setting anything up.

resources.title: Happy Git and GitHub for the useR
resources.provider: Jenny Bryan, the STAT 545 TAs, Jim Hester
resources.blurb: Best-in-class onboarding to Git for researchers, especially those using R. Sets up the toolchain step-by-step and explains 'why' not just 'how'.

resources.title: The Turing Way
resources.blurb: An open handbook on reproducible, ethical, collaborative data science. Use it as a reference for project structure, FAIR data, and open research practice.

resources.title: Software & Data Carpentry lessons
resources.blurb: Volunteer-maintained workshop materials covering shell, Git, Python/R, and SQL for researchers. Excellent self-study and even better as the basis for an in-house workshop.

resources.title: Galaxy Training Network
resources.blurb: Hundreds of GUI-driven, runnable tutorials covering RNA-seq, variant calling, proteomics, single-cell, machine learning, and more. The fastest path into bioinformatics for non-coders.

resources.title: Bioconductor — Course Materials
resources.blurb: Annual workshops and labs from the R/Bioconductor ecosystem. Authoritative for omics analysis in R.

resources.title: Rosalind — Bioinformatics problems
resources.blurb: Project-Euler-style bioinformatics challenges. Great for building Python fluency on biological data without committing to a long course.

resources.title: Biopython Tutorial & Cookbook
resources.blurb: Working with sequences, alignments, structures, and biological databases in Python. Complements Galaxy when you need scripted control.

resources.title: RDKit Cookbook
resources.blurb: Code recipes for the standard cheminformatics toolkit — SMILES parsing, fingerprints, similarity, structure search, descriptors, conformers.

resources.title: TeachOpenCADD
resources.blurb: A complete computer-aided drug design course as Jupyter notebooks. Covers ligand-based + structure-based design, ADMET, and ML for drug discovery — all reusable as teaching material.

resources.title: DeepChem Tutorials
resources.blurb: Step-by-step notebooks for ML on molecules — featurisation, regression, classification, generative models. Pairs cheminformatics with deep learning.

resources.title: Practical Deep Learning for Coders
resources.blurb: Top-down deep learning course that has you training real models in week 1 and explaining them in week 7. The 'Deep Learning for Coders' book that accompanies it is also free as Jupyter notebooks.

resources.title: Hugging Face NLP / LLM Course
resources.blurb: End-to-end course on transformers, fine-tuning, datasets, and evaluation. The standard introduction to modern NLP/LLM tooling.

resources.title: Made With ML
resources.blurb: Goes beyond model training into MLOps — testing, CI/CD, monitoring, and shipping ML systems responsibly. Useful when you need a model to actually run somewhere.

resources.title: Machine Learning Specialization
resources.blurb: Andrew Ng's reboot of the classic Coursera ML course. Audit free; pay (~£40/month) for graded assignments and a certificate.

resources.title: AI for Medicine Specialization
resources.blurb: Three courses on diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment with ML — covers medical image segmentation, survival analysis, and causal inference for medicine. Closest 'pharma-flavoured' specialization at this level.

resources.title: mrgsolve — User Guide & Vignettes
resources.blurb: Open R package for population PK/PD simulation. Vignettes are the de-facto introduction to model-based simulation in R.

resources.title: Open pharmacometrics teaching materials (Bauer / Mould lectures, NMUsersGroup archives)
resources.format: Mixed (lecture notes, NMUsers archive, vendor docs)
resources.blurb: There is no single canonical open pharmacometrics course. The community lives in NMUsers archives, vendor tutorial decks (ICON/NONMEM, Lixoft/Monolix), and conference workshops. Treat this as an entry point — ask a CPDSE pharmacometrician for a curated path.

resources.title: Pharmacometrics — Coursera (University of Cape Town)
resources.blurb: One of the few formal MOOCs on pharmacometrics. Audit-free or pay for a certificate.

resources.title: Fundamentals of Data Visualization
resources.blurb: Principles-first guide to making honest, readable charts. Tool-agnostic; the principles port to ggplot2, Plotly, matplotlib alike.

resources.title: ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis (3rd ed.)
resources.provider: Hadley Wickham, Danielle Navarro, Thomas Lin Pedersen
resources.blurb: The reference book for ggplot2. Goes deep on the grammar of graphics and the customisation patterns you need for publication-quality figures.

resources.title: Python Data Science Handbook
resources.blurb: NumPy, pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, and scikit-learn — the canonical reference for the core scientific-Python stack.

resources.title: Datawrapper Academy
resources.blurb: Tool-agnostic chart-design articles by Lisa Charlotte Muth. Excellent treatment of how to choose a chart type and label it well.

resources.title: HarvardX Data Science Professional Certificate
resources.blurb: Audit each course for free; pay for the certificate. Covers R, viz, probability, inference, ML, and a capstone. The most-recommended single learning path for stats + ML in R.

resources.title: MITx MicroMasters in Statistics & Data Science
resources.blurb: Rigorous graduate-level treatment of probability, stats, ML, and capstone. Audit free; verified track requires payment per course. Stackable into MIT degrees.

resources.title: Statistical Learning with R / Python
resources.blurb: The video lectures companion to the ISLR book, taught by the authors. Audit free or pay for the verified certificate.

resources.title: AI in Healthcare Specialization
resources.blurb: Stanford School of Medicine's overview of AI in healthcare — from data fundamentals through evaluation and ethics. Most pharma-relevant of the Stanford Coursera offerings.

resources.title: Data Science Specialization
resources.blurb: Long-running R-based specialisation that covers tooling, EDA, regression models, ML, and a capstone. Stackable; audit-free per course.

resources.title: Genomic Data Science Specialization
resources.blurb: Covers Galaxy, Bioconductor, Python for genomics, statistics, and a capstone. Strong applied bioinformatics path.

resources.title: Mathematics for Machine Learning Specialization
resources.blurb: Linear algebra, multivariate calculus, and PCA, framed for ML. Bridge between 3Blue1Brown intuition and reading actual ML papers.

resources.title: Applied Data Science with Python Specialization
resources.blurb: Pandas, plotting, ML with scikit-learn, text analysis, network analysis. Practical, applied; assumes you know basic Python (do Python for Everybody first).

resources.title: Microsoft Learn — Machine Learning paths
resources.blurb: Free, hands-on learning paths with optional paid Microsoft Certified credentials at the end (Azure Data Scientist Associate is the relevant one). Useful if your org uses Azure.

resources.title: FDA — Real-World Evidence Program
resources.blurb: Primary-source FDA guidance on RWE / RWD methodology. The framework most pharma data work eventually has to align to.

resources.title: EMA — Big Data and Artificial Intelligence
resources.blurb: EMA's published reflection papers and the EMA-FDA joint AI principles. The European counterpart to FDA RWE guidance.

resources.title: FAIRsharing — Standards, Databases, Policies
resources.blurb: Find the right metadata standard, database, or policy for your data. Essential for FAIR-compliant data sharing in life science.

resources.title: GDPR for researchers — UCPH guidance
resources.blurb: UCPH's research-facing GDPR pages — DPA templates, data classifications, what to do with patient data. Start here when a project will touch personal data.

resources.title: Kaggle Learn
resources.blurb: Bite-sized free courses on pandas, ML, computer vision, time series, geospatial. Best as a refresher between bigger commitments.

resources.title: OpenML — open ML data + benchmarks
resources.blurb: Open repository of curated ML datasets and benchmark results. Useful when you need a teaching dataset that's not the same iris/MNIST as everyone else.
